heading: Limitation on deductions allocable to property used by governments or other tax-exempt entities
label: 470 Limitation on deductions allocable to property used by governments or other tax-exempt entities
notes: Amendments2018—Subsec. (d)(2)(B). substituted “subparagraph (A)(ii)” for “clause (ii)”.Pub. L. 115–1412007—Subsec. (c)(2). , reenacted heading without change and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The term ‘tax-exempt use property’ has the meaning given to such term by section 168(h), except that such section shall be applied—Pub. L. 110–172, § 7(c)(1)“(A) without regard to paragraphs (1)(C) and (3) thereof, and“(B) as if property described in—“(i) section 167(f)(1)(B),“(ii) section 167(f)(2), and“(iii) section 197 intangible,were tangible property.Such term shall not include property which would (but for this sentence) be tax-exempt use property solely by reason of section 168(h)(6) if any credit is allowable under section 42 or 47 with respect to such property.”Subsec. (d)(1)(A). , in introductory provisions, substituted “(at all times during the lease term)” for “(at any time during the lease term)”.Pub. L. 110–172, § 7(c)(2)

children.heading: Limitation on losses
children.label: a Limitation on losses
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this section, a tax-exempt use loss for any taxable year shall not be allowed.

children.heading: Disallowed loss carried to next year
children.label: b Disallowed loss carried to next year
children.text: Any tax-exempt use loss with respect to any tax-exempt use property which is disallowed under subsection (a) for any taxable year shall be treated as a deduction with respect to such property in the next taxable year.

children.label: i
children.text: the aggregate deductions (other than interest) directly allocable to a tax-exempt use property, plus

children.label: ii
children.text: the aggregate deductions for interest properly allocable to such property, exceed

children.label: B
children.text: the aggregate income from such property.

children.label: i
children.text: without regard to paragraphs (1)(C) and (3) thereof, and

children.label: ii
children.text: as if section 197 intangible property (as defined in section 197), and property described in paragraph (1)(B) or (2) of section 167(f), were tangible property.

children.heading: Exception for partnerships
children.label: B Exception for partnerships
children.text: Such term shall not include any property which would (but for this subparagraph) be tax-exempt use property solely by reason of section 168(h)(6).

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: C Cross reference
children.text: For treatment of partnerships as leases to which section 168(h) applies, see section 7701(e).

children.label: i
children.text: subject to any arrangement referred to in subparagraph (B), or

children.label: ii
children.text: set aside or expected to be set aside,

children.heading: Arrangements
children.label: B Arrangements
children.text: The arrangements referred to in this subparagraph include a defeasance arrangement, a loan by the lessee to the lessor or any lender, a deposit arrangement, a letter of credit collateralized with cash or cash equivalents, a payment undertaking agreement, prepaid rent (within the meaning of the regulations under section 467), a sinking fund arrangement, a guaranteed investment contract, financial guaranty insurance, and any similar arrangement (whether or not such arrangement provides credit support).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this subparagraph, the term “allowable amount” means an amount equal to 20 percent of the lessor’s adjusted basis in the property at the time the lease is entered into.

children.heading: Higher amount permitted in certain cases
children.label: ii Higher amount permitted in certain cases
children.text: To the extent provided in regulations, a higher percentage shall be permitted under clause (i) where necessary because of the credit-worthiness of the lessee. In no event may such regulations permit a percentage of more than 50 percent.

children.heading: Option to purchase
children.label: iii Option to purchase
children.text: If under the lease the lessee has the option to purchase the property for a fixed price or for other than the fair market value of the property (determined at the time of exercise), the allowable amount at the time such option may be exercised may not exceed 50 percent of the price at which such option may be exercised.

children.heading: No allowable amount for certain arrangements
children.label: iv No allowable amount for certain arrangements
I
children.text: a loan from the lessee to the lessor or a lender,

children.label: II
children.text: any deposit received, letter of credit issued, or payment undertaking agreement entered into by a lender otherwise involved in the transaction, or

children.label: III
children.text: in the case of a transaction which involves a lender, any credit support made available to the lessor in which any such lender does not have a claim that is senior to the lessor.

children.heading: Lessor must make substantial equity investment
children.label: 2 Lessor must make substantial equity investment
I
children.text: has at the time the lease is entered into an unconditional at-risk equity investment (as determined by the Secretary) in the property of at least 20 percent of the lessor’s adjusted basis in the property as of that time, and

children.label: II
children.text: maintains such investment throughout the term of the lease, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the fair market value of the property at the end of the lease term is reasonably expected to be equal to at least 20 percent of such basis.

children.heading: Risk of loss
children.label: B Risk of loss
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A)(ii), the fair market value at the end of the lease term shall be reduced to the extent that a person other than the lessor bears a risk of loss in the value of the property.

children.heading: Paragraph not to apply to short-term leases
children.label: C Paragraph not to apply to short-term leases
children.text: This paragraph shall not apply to any lease with a lease term of 5 years or less.

children.heading: Lessee may not bear more than minimal risk of loss
children.label: 3 Lessee may not bear more than minimal risk of loss
i
children.text: any portion of the loss that would occur if the fair market value of the leased property were 25 percent less than its reasonably expected fair market value at the time the lease is terminated, or

children.label: ii
children.text: more than 50 percent of the loss that would occur if the fair market value of the leased property at the time the lease is terminated were zero.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: The Secretary may by regulations provide that the requirements of this paragraph are not met where the lessee bears more than a minimal risk of loss.

children.heading: Paragraph not to apply to short-term leases
children.label: C Paragraph not to apply to short-term leases
children.text: This paragraph shall not apply to any lease with a lease term of 5 years or less.

children.heading: Property with more than 7-year class life
children.label: 4 Property with more than 7-year class life
A
children.text: of property with a class life (as defined in section 168(i)(1)) of more than 7 years, other than fixed-wing aircraft and vessels, and

children.label: B
children.text: under which the lessee has the option to purchase the property,

children.heading: Treatment of former tax-exempt use property
children.label: 1 Treatment of former tax-exempt use property
i
children.text: any deduction allowable under subsection (b) with respect to such property for any taxable year shall be allowed only to the extent of any net income (without regard to such deduction) from such property for such taxable year, and

children.label: ii
children.text: any portion of such unused deduction remaining after application of clause (i) shall be treated as a deduction allowable under subsection (b) with respect to such property in the next taxable year.

children.label: i
children.text: is not tax-exempt use property for the taxable year, but

children.label: ii
children.text: was tax-exempt use property for any prior taxable year.

children.heading: Disposition of entire interest in property
children.label: 2 Disposition of entire interest in property
children.text: If during the taxable year a taxpayer disposes of the taxpayer’s entire interest in tax-exempt use property (or former tax-exempt use property), rules similar to the rules of section 469(g) shall apply for purposes of this section.

children.heading: Coordination with section 469
children.label: 3 Coordination with section 469
children.text: This section shall be applied before the application of section 469.

children.label: i
children.text: the exchanged or converted property is tax-exempt use property subject to a lease which was entered into before , and which would not have met the requirements of subsection (d) had such requirements been in effect when the lease was entered into, orMarch 13, 2004

children.label: ii
children.text: the replacement property is tax-exempt use property subject to a lease which does not meet the requirements of subsection (d).

children.label: i
children.text: the fair market value of the property as of the beginning of the lease term, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount which would be the lessor’s adjusted basis if such sections did not apply to such transaction.

children.heading: Related parties
children.label: 1 Related parties
children.text: The terms “lessor”, “lessee”, and “lender” each include any related party (within the meaning of section 197(f)(9)(C)(i)).

children.heading: Lease term
children.label: 2 Lease term
children.text: The term “lease term” has the meaning given to such term by section 168(i)(3).

children.heading: Lender
children.label: 3 Lender
children.text: The term “lender” means, with respect to any lease, a person that makes a loan to the lessor which is secured (or economically similar to being secured) by the lease or the leased property.

children.heading: Loan
children.label: 4 Loan
children.text: The term “loan” includes any similar arrangement.
allow in appropriate cases the aggregation of property subject to the same lease, and
provide for the determination of the allocation of interest expense for purposes of this section.
